key_insight: URSA is a chemistry-aware benchmarking framework that compares retrosynthesis routes by both formal feasibility and expert-like chemical plausibility, revealing that specialized models currently outperform LLMs on realistic synthesis-planning tasks.
clinical_relevance: By improving how drug-chemistry synthesis planning is assessed, this framework could help prioritize more reliable route-design tools for medicinal chemistry and therapeutic discovery workflows.
method: Benchmarked end-to-end retrosynthesis systems and general-purpose LLMs on novel target molecules with undisclosed routes.
Scored routes using both convergence to commercially available starting materials and chemical plausibility.
Designed the evaluation to mimic how expert chemists judge reaction and route quality.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a comprehensive evaluation on diverse, novel targets and finds LLMs are better for high-level strategy but less reliable than specialized retrosynthesis models.
watch_item: Because the benchmark is still an evaluation framework rather than a direct synthesis solution, results may depend on how plausibility scoring is implemented and interpreted.
paper_title: URSA: Chemistry-Aware Benchmark for Utilitarian Retrosynthesis Assessment
